title: Nothapodytes gardneriana
intro: Nothapodytes gardneriana, also known as the Indian Mastich or Indian Catechu, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of N. gardneriana include catechin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly processed into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Nothapodytes gardneriana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is native to the Western Ghats of India and is also known as the Indian staff tree. The plant is a small tree or shrub with small, yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is a member of the genus Nothapodytes, which consists of several species of trees and shrubs. Nothapodytes gardneriana is often found growing in forests and woodlands, where it can tolerate a range of conditions.

side_effects: Nothapodytes gardneriana, also known as the Indian walnut, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
